beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make espresso and toast at home.
beats.reasoning: Friday morning ritual—Tessa starts her day with a quiet espresso moment before heading to work. Yesterday ended low-energy (45/100), so she's keeping this beat focused and grounding.
Morning commute to the coffee shop. I-90 to WA-520 westbound, typical Friday traffic but manageable. Arrives in time for opening prep.

beats.activity: Open the shop, train staff, pull espresso, manage morning rush.
beats.reasoning: Shift lead duties on a warm Friday morning. The warm weather (77F) will drive more iced drink orders. Tessa handles opening, barista training, and the Friday morning crowd with her characteristic focus.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and iced coffee nearby.
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch at the shop between rushes—Tessa eats efficiently during her shift, staying close to work. Fuels up for the afternoon.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift: espresso service, inventory check, prepping for close.; Close the shop, count register, restock for tomorrow.
beats.reasoning: The warm Friday afternoon is typically quieter post-lunch. Tessa uses the time to handle inventory, reset the bar, and prep for evening close. Organized and methodical as always.

beats.activity: Drive from Kirkland toward Capitol Hill, avoiding Pride road closures on Broadway and Pike.
beats.reasoning: Tessa heads toward Capitol Hill for the evening. The Pride street fair starts at 5pm with closures on Broadway and Pike. She plans her route via side streets (E Pine, E Olive) to avoid the main gridlock. Arrives around 6:25pm.

beats.activity: Walk through Capitol Hill Pride street fair, grab street food, soak in the festive energy with a close friend.
beats.reasoning: Tessa values time with close friends over big crowds, but Pride is a cherished community event. She meets a friend, enjoys the warm evening, samples local vendors, and takes a few photos with her camera. Not overwhelming—just a pleasant Friday evening out.

beats.activity: Drive back to Bellevue, taking I-5 south to I-90 east to avoid lingering Pride closures.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute home. Tessa navigates around the Pride street fair closures by using I-5 and I-90, arriving home around 8:15pm.

beats.activity: Unwind at home: review film photos on laptop, sip tea, update expense spreadsheet.
beats.reasoning: Tessa winds down with her usual evening ritual. The warm day and social outing energized her, so she settles into solo time with her camera work and spreadsheets—her grounding rituals. This prepares her for sleep.
Full night's rest after a solid Friday. The day balanced work discipline, community connection, and personal ritual—exactly the kind of day Tessa plans for.

diary:
Woke up early to the smell of my own espresso—one of the small luxuries of being shift lead. The drive to Kirkland was smooth, and I felt ready for the morning rush. We had a new barista shadowing me, so I spent time showing her our pull technique. My hands remember the motions better than my brain does at 8 AM.

Lunch was quiet. I grabbed a sandwich and sat in the back with my iced coffee, just breathing for twenty minutes.

After close, I made a last-minute decision to head into Capitol Hill for Pride. The energy was infectious—rainbow flags everywhere, music spilling from different corners, people genuinely happy. I met up with Jordan, and we wandered through the street fair, grabbed some food, laughed at vendors. It felt good to be around that kind of joy, even as an observer.

Drove back through the sunset feeling lighter than I have in weeks.
thought_process: Today felt like a balance I needed. Work was solid and routine—I like training people, even when it's exhausting. The Pride event wasn't planned, really; I just didn't want to go straight home. There's something about being around community celebration that reminds me I'm not just moving through the world alone, even if I'm not always at the center of things. The weather helped too. That warmth and breeze made everything feel possible. I navigated around the road closures without frustration because, honestly, seeing the city come alive for something joyful felt important, even from the periphery. By evening, I was tired but settled—the kind of tired that comes from being present somewhere.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
